rt1-2-2.title: Hands-on: beat Ir-192 decay with exposure time
rt1-2-2.intro: Expose film with a fresh 40 Ci Ir-192 source until density lands in the 2.0-3.0 window, then jump 30 days ahead: activity has fallen along A = A0 x 2^(-t/73.8), so you must stretch the exposure time to win the density back - the same half-life math as this lesson's decay tables.

tasks.label: Expose film into the 2.0-3.0 density window
tasks.hint: Density tracks total exposure E = activity x time - nudge the time slider and watch the density readout climb.

tasks.label: After 30 days of decay, recover the density
tasks.hint: 30 days is about 0.4 half-lives: activity is down to ~75%, so exposure time must grow by roughly a third.

rt1-2-3.title: Hands-on: time, distance, shielding vs gamma photons
rt1-2-3.intro: Gamma photons cross the whole bay and no shield stops them all - lead only thins the field. Work the three defenses this lesson ends on: back away (inverse square), stack half-value layers, and cap the time you spend in the field.

tasks.label: Double your distance and watch the rate fall 4x
tasks.hint: Photons spread over a sphere, so intensity goes as 1/d-squared: twice the distance means one quarter the rate.

tasks.label: Stack lead until the boundary reads under 2 mR/h
tasks.hint: Each half-value layer halves the rate - count the halvings needed to get from ~86 mR/h down below 2.

tasks.label: Plan a 10-minute job under the 20 mrem budget
tasks.hint: Dose = rate x time; if the plan busts the budget, lower the rate first with distance or another slab.

rt1-2-4.title: Hands-on: Co-60 - when the nucleus sets the energy
rt1-2-4.intro: Swap the bay's source for Co-60. Its 1.17 and 1.33 MeV lines are nuclear constants - no kV or mA knob - so the HUD's output (1.32 R-m2/Ci-h vs Ir-192's 0.48) and 12.5 mm lead HVL are simply what you must work around: shield the boundary, then budget your dose.

tasks.label: Shield the boundary - count the HVLs Co-60 costs
tasks.hint: At Co-60 energy one half-value layer is 12.5 mm of lead; expect about six slabs to get under 2 mR/h.

tasks.label: Budget a 15-minute shot under 20 mrem
tasks.hint: Output is activity, not a dial: dose = rate x time, so buy your margin with distance and lead before adding minutes.
